qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via webhook target URL allows authenticated users to reach internal services
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns webhook TargetURL validation and server-side POST delivery in pkg/models/webhooks.go, while the advisory concerns task-preview XSS from an unescaped task description assigned to innerHTML in TaskGlanceTooltip.vue.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a Vikunja task preview XSS caused by assigning an unescaped task description to innerHTML in TaskGlanceTooltip.vue and triggering execution on hover; the only final qualifying finding is an unrelated webhook SSRF.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-m4g2-2q66-vc9v describes TaskGlanceTooltip.vue creating an unparented div and setting its innerHTML to an unescaped task description.
The final finding manifest lists only webhook_ssrf as a qualifying_vuln finding.

webhook_ssrf reports TargetURL validation using only an http prefix check and server-side POST delivery through the webhook pipeline, not task description rendering, TaskGlanceTooltip.vue, innerHTML assignment, or hover-triggered XSS.
